senses.definition: to be able to do something wrong or illegal without receiving punishment or facing any consequences
to unfairly try to do something when it is someone else's turn to do so
a situation where someone's personal stake or potential gain from a decision may hinder their ability to make an unbiased or fair choice

examples.x: The judge recused himself from the case due to a conflict of interest, as he was a shareholder in the company being sued.
The sports coach faced allegations of favoritism and a potential conflict of interest when it was discovered that a family member was on the team.
Her role as a journalist clashed with her personal friendship with the politician, resulting in a conflict of interest.
The researcher's close family connection to a study subject prompted concerns about a conflict of interest in the research.

senses.definition: a treatment that is not fair or equal

examples.x: I feel like I'm getting a raw deal in this business partnership.
She always gets a raw deal when it comes to promotions at work.
We're constantly being given a raw deal by our landlord.
He got a raw deal in that lawsuit; the settlement was far less than he deserved.
They received a raw deal during the negotiation process and were left with unfavorable terms.
The employees felt they had been given a raw deal when their benefits were suddenly cut.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈɑːɹ ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ wɪððə sˈeɪm bˈʊʃ

senses.definition: to unfairly assume that a person or thing is as same as someone or something that they are associated with
a comment or statement that is cruel or unfair

examples.x: He enabled him to take the cheap shot that ended the fight, stirred the pot and kept Mayweather at the center of controversy.
I didn't appreciate that cheap shot you took at me at the party.
I think her making fun of his wife was a cheap shot
It would be a cheap shot to roast Franklin with the evidence he gave you as his friend.

senses.definition: to express a strong objection to something that is perceived as unfair or unjust
an unofficial court that does not follow the rules and usually punishes people without giving them a fair trial

examples.x: They are conducting a kangaroo court to quickly dispense with the accused's case.
The employees believe that the disciplinary hearings at their workplace are akin to a kangaroo court.
The activists claim that the government is running a kangaroo court to suppress dissenting voices.
The defendant's lawyer argued that the trial was a kangaroo court due to the biased judge.
We knew it was a kangaroo court as the verdict was reached before any evidence was presented.
The international community condemned the dictator's regime for their kangaroo courts during that period.
If the authorities continue to ignore due process, there will be a risk of turning the legal system into a kangaroo court.
I fear that the company's internal investigation might devolve into a kangaroo court if proper safeguards are not in place.

senses.definition: to receive special treatment or permission to avoid obligations, restrictions, or consequences

examples.x: He always gets a free pass for his mistakes while others face consequences.
They get a free pass because of their influential connections.
She got a free pass for her tardiness because of her close relationship with the boss.
Despite the evidence against him, he somehow got a free pass in the trial.

senses.definition: in a way that unfairly takes advantage of a person's weaknesses

examples.x: Bringing up her past failures during the argument was a below the belt move.
Spreading false rumors about his personal life was a below the belt tactic to tarnish his reputation.
His sarcastic comment about her appearance was a below the belt remark that crossed the line.
Using confidential information against a former colleague in a business deal was a below the belt action.
Mocking someone's insecurities in front of others is hitting below the belt.
Insulting a person's family during a debate is considered below the belt in a civilized discussion.
Sending anonymous hate messages to exploit someone's vulnerabilities is a below the belt act of cyberbullying.
Employing personal attacks instead of addressing the issue at hand is resorting to below the belt strategies.

senses.definition: a person or group who has unchecked power to make decisions, impose punishments, and carry out those punishments without due process or oversight

word_list.word: the short end of the stick
word_list.phonetic_text: ðə ʃɔrt ɛnd ʌv ðə stɪk
word_list.phonetic_am_text: dhē shawrt end av dhē stik

senses.definition: an unfavorable or disadvantageous position or outcome that one receives in a particular situation
